steps.title: Trace the passage's structure
steps.content: The passage moves in three beats: (1) it briefly describes the 2020 reissue project, (2) it introduces Rutigliano and other scholars and their critical stance toward that project, and (3) it expresses support for that stance by explaining what the project lacks (nuance) and why (it ignores elective self-fashioning). Hold that three-part map — description, critical stance, support — against the choices.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'A literary project is briefly described, the stance taken by Rutigliano and others in response to that project is introduced, and then support for that stance is expressed.': Matches all three beats exactly: project described, Rutigliano's critical stance introduced, support for that stance given. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Details about a reissue of *The Silence of Dean Maitland* are given, a question about the authorship of that work is raised, and then the circumstances of the work's original publication are considered.': Invents an authorship question and original-publication discussion — neither appears in the passage. ✗
  • Choice C — '*The Silence of Dean Maitland* is cited as a prominent work from a literary movement, a study of the movement's defining features is mentioned, and then one of the study's insights is presented.': Invents a "literary movement" frame; the passage is about a reissue project and scholars' critique, not a movement study. ✗
  • Choice D — 'An assertion about a literary project is reported, the team of scholars that advanced the assertion is identified, and then the reasoning behind the assertion is summarized.': Misrepresents the scholars: in the passage Rutigliano et al. critique the project's assertion, they do not advance it. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
